channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Sofa left on street, 2nd March
item.description:
Sofa left on street. Probably by resident of 54a

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Shenley Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 potholes near 106. One is 3 inches deep., 2nd March
item.description:
Not marked up. Difficult for vehicles to avoid

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping - Multiple bags of rubbish and large box - health issue, 2nd March
item.description:
Bags of rubbish were dumped and nothing has been done to clear them. There is a pungent smell emitting from the rubbish so this is likely to attract foxes and or rats quickly becoming a health hazard and rubbish being strewn all over the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole near traffic island B52, 2nd March
item.description:
Not marked up

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Hall Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Designated Parking Boxes, 2nd March
item.description:
The designated marked out pavement parking boxes in Highbanks Close DA16 3ES , have over many years, almost completely worn away. Last week I received a ticket for parking on the pavement, whilst parked in a designated box. Luckily I caught the parking attendant and pointed out the almost non existent markings and he removed and canceled the ticket ... This is not acceptable and the designated boxes need remarking asap or sooner ...
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Highbanks Close, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This report is regarding a Sofa that was dumped in front of my property by a disfunctional and disgruntled neighbour., 2nd March
item.description:
The sofa was moved from a convenient location to the pavement in front of my property in an act of provocation. The sofa has nothing whatsoever to do with me. The name of the person in the images is John who lives in one of the flats known as The Studios, St James's Road, Bermondsey London, SE1 5JX Source of evidence is my CCTV time and date stamped images. Video evidence is available upon request. Please prosecute this person as a deterrent to others.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St James's Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The gate has been broken for months, 2nd March
item.description:
The gate has been broken for months on end, this needs to be fixed immediately as it is a securiy issue. EACH TIME IT NEEDS TO BE FIXED, WE AS LEASEHOLDERS PAY MORE. People are accessing the property that don't live here.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Valette Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple large potholes, 2nd March
item.description:
Multiple large potholes along this road. If you are driving down towards FootsCray, you have to swerve onto opposite side of road to avoid the craters in the road and to avoid damaging your car! Extremely dangerous as you have to illegally drive on opposite side of road. If a cyclist or motorbike hits these at night (especially as lighting is so poor on this road), they will be seriously injured.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rectory Lane, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside J Murphy and Sons Hiview House, Highgate Road, London, NW5 1TN, 2nd March
item.description:
Multiple bollards damaged
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carker's Lane, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep potholes formed on either side of both speed bumps along this carriageway, 2nd March
item.description:
Deep potholes formed on either side of both speed bumps along this carriageway. The whole gutterside of the carriageway is potholed along this section. Absolutely lethal for cyclists, particularly at night - cars try to squeeze you into the curb to get around the island
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wood Vale, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Square pot hole in bike lane by bus stop, 2nd March
item.description:
My bike had difficulty with the pot hole

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East End Road, Church End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clothes thrown all over the sidewalk, 2nd March
item.description:
It looks like someone left clothes for the clothes bank and someone else tore open the bags and threw the clothes all over the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Park, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Late Sunday night this car has been parked in front of my house due to which I was unable to take out my car, 2nd March
item.description:
Car has been front of my driveway rendering me unable to take my car out. I waited till 8:45 AM leave to my office. But due to this car I was unable to take my car out. And had to walk to my office 1 mile away

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Victoria Road, Warley, Brentwood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside St Peter church Belsize square, 2nd March
item.description:
I am writing to formally raise a serious health and safety complaint regarding construction works taking place at Belsize Square, under the jurisdiction of Camden Council. The works are being carried out by Parkview Development Group Ltd at St Peter’s Church. The contractor placed large, heavy metal plates on the paved area and left them unsecured and uncovered. On 12/02/2026 at approximately 7:50pm, the sharp exposed edge of one of these plates struck my vehicle tyre, causing it to burst and resulting in damage to the alloy wheel. This situation presents a serious public safety hazard. The unsecured metal plates pose a clear risk of severe injury to pedestrians, cyclists, and particularly children. Someone could easily slip, fall, or suffer life-threatening injuries due to this negligence. Despite reporting the matter directly to the company and providing evidence of the tyre and wheel damage along with repair costs, they have failed to: Assist with my claim Provide their insurance details Accept responsibility Adequately secure the site in a timely manner I understand that construction sites must comply with strict safety regulations. I therefore request that Camden Council: Investigate this matter urgently. Require the company to provide a valid public liability insurance certificate. Ensure the site is fully secured in accordance with health and safety guidelines. Consider appropriate enforcement action, including penalties if breaches are found. Confirm whether the council was aware of these unsafe conditions. As a taxpayer, I also expect the Council to ensure proper oversight once planning permission or construction approval has been granted. Public safety must remain the priority. Please confirm receipt of this complaint and inform me of the next steps. Please find attached pictures I look forward to your urgent response. Yours faithfully, Muhammad Ahmed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belsize Square, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping at bottom of Roydene Road, near Slade ponds, 2nd March
item.description:
Flytipping of household rubbish. Split open by foxes or cats as usual and now all over the road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Roydene Road, Plumstead

item.title: Rubbish along Millenium Way, 2nd March
item.description:
Rubbish including remains of food vendo, pamphlets and drug paraphernalia along Millenium Way. More rubbish bins required on this street, especially on days Studio 338 hosts events.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Millennium Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Residents at back of the shops and shops dumping household waste next to the telephone box. e, 2nd March
item.description:
This is happening every week. Not nice 4 the residents in our street. I have reported this many times even showing rubbish with the address on. Please take this serious and take action.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Langley Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Zip ties and detritus left on traffic lights and ground, 2nd March
item.description:
This report follows previous issue managed by TfL, copied below for reference. Posters have now been removed but the maintenance team has left zip ties on some traffic lights and cut zip tie detritus on the ground. Fly posting on traffic lights Reported via mobile in the Graffiti / Flyposting on traffic light (non-offensive) category anonymously at 16:39, Saturday 28 February 2026 using FixMyStreet Pro Sent to TfL less than a minute later. FixMyStreet ref: 9018510. Photo of this report Multiple posters and zip ties on traffics lights

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackwall Tunnel Approach, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flat bins - mattress dumped, 2nd March
item.description:
Mattress flytipped at flat bins
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Austen Close, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
